tasks.label: Play: Build + Serve (all-in-one)
tasks.detail: Builds the frontend, serves the game on http://localhost:8080, and opens the browser

tasks.label: Play: Serve + open browser
tasks.detail: Runs the backend and pops the browser once the server answers
dependsOn: Browser: Open when server is ready

tasks.label: Play with paddle: Build + Serve
tasks.detail: Like Play, but routes every hit's haptics to the real ESP32 paddle (auto-learns its IP from telemetry)
dependsOn: Play with paddle: Serve + open browser

tasks.label: Play with paddle: Serve + open browser
tasks.detail: Runs the backend aimed at the real paddle, then opens the browser
dependsOn: Browser: Open when server is ready

tasks.label: Play with REAL paddle (IMU): Build + Serve
tasks.detail: Everything for hardware mode: build the frontend, run the backend so the real paddle's MPU6050 drives the on-screen paddle, route haptics to it, and open the browser. Hold the paddle still ~2 s at start to zero the gyro; press C in the browser to recenter 'flat'.
dependsOn: Play with REAL paddle (IMU): Serve + open browser

tasks.label: Play with REAL paddle (IMU): Serve + open browser
tasks.detail: Runs the backend in hardware mode (IMU drives orientation), then opens the browser
dependsOn: Browser: Open when server is ready

tasks.label: Browser: Open when server is ready
tasks.detail: Waits for http://localhost:8080 to come up (max 30 s), then opens it
args: $deadline = (Get-Date).AddSeconds(30); $up = $false; while ((Get-Date) -lt $deadline) { try { Invoke-WebRequest -UseBasicParsing -TimeoutSec 1 http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/health | Out-Null; $up = $true; break } catch { Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 500 } }; if ($up) { Write-Host 'Server up - opening browser'; Start-Process 'http://127.0.0.1:8080' } else { Write-Host 'Server did not come up within 30 s - not opening browser' }

tasks.label: Backend: Run (sim)
tasks.detail: Start the authoritative game backend with mock camera + paddle on :8080

tasks.label: Backend: Run (paddle haptics)
tasks.detail: Backend aimed at the real ESP32 paddle: mock paddle off, haptics auto-routed to whichever paddle is streaming telemetry

tasks.label: Backend: Run (real paddle IMU)
tasks.detail: Hardware mode: no mock camera/paddle. The real paddle's IMU drives orientation (position fixed), haptics auto-route back to it. Watch the log for 'POSE SOURCE: real paddle IMU'.

tasks.label: Frontend: Build
tasks.detail: Typecheck + Vite production build into frontend/dist (served by the backend)

tasks.label: Frontend: Dev server (hot reload)
tasks.detail: Vite dev server on :5173, proxying /api and /ws to the backend on :8080

tasks.label: Dev: Backend + Frontend (hot reload)
tasks.detail: Run both servers for development — open http://localhost:5173

tasks.label: Backend: Tests
tasks.detail: Run the full pytest suite (44 tests)

tasks.label: Backend: Headless demo
tasks.detail: Full pipeline session in ~10 s with no browser; prints impacts + metrics

tasks.label: Fix: Kill stale backend (frees UDP 46000 / HTTP 8080)
tasks.detail: Use when metrics show paddleConnected=false after a restart
args: $pids = @(); try { $pids += (Get-NetUDPEndpoint -LocalPort 46000 -ErrorAction Stop).OwningProcess } catch {}; try { $pids += (Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 8080 -State Listen -ErrorAction Stop).OwningProcess } catch {}; $pids = $pids | Sort-Object -Unique | Where-Object { $_ -gt 0 }; if ($pids) { $pids | ForEach-Object { Write-Host "Killing PID $_"; Stop-Process -Id $_ -Force } } else { Write-Host 'Nothing holding 46000/8080 — all clear.' }
